data.narrative:
Forterra, founded by Alberto Lacaze, is a Series B–stage defense and security technology company that has raised $228M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 10 comparable companies—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms operating in the same sector and stage. The private peer set includes Dream Security, Harmatan AI, Code Metal, Castelion, and Defense Unicorns, among others.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding $433M. Peer-median analysis across 7 same-sector, same-stage comparables produces a $1.0B implied valuation; the broader peer range spans $300M to $3.0B, reflecting material heterogeneity in deal quality and investor conviction within the cohort.

The two methods triangulate to a valuation range of $368M (low), $717M (median), and $1.1B (high). The median estimate of $717M sits roughly midway between the stage-progression floor and the peer median ceiling. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of stage-progression multipliers given Forterra's specific capital structure and technical differentiation relative to the peer set, as well as the timing and selectivity of recent comparable transactions, which may not reflect current market conditions in defense-tech valuations.
